title: Graph-template apply engine
description:
Add the engine that instantiates a graph template onto a container.

Validate all preconditions and snapshot each anchor's dependencies before any mutation; create the template's nodes with interpolated descriptions and doc locations; attach each node's declared gate presets; wire internal and anchor edges; run the move-upstream-to-role transform against the pre-apply snapshot. Coexists with the existing scaffold path.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-01: Applying a template creates its nodes with interpolated, non-empty descriptions and resolved doc locations.
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-02: Internal and anchor edges are wired; the result is acyclic and transitively reduced.
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-03: move-upstream-to-role moves the container's pre-apply dependencies onto the named node, never the freshly-created scaffold nodes.
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-04: A precondition failure (bad anchor, wrong container type, cycle) creates zero nodes.
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-05: Each created node has its template-declared gate presets attached to its required gates.
- [ ] [hard] APPLY-06: Applying a template returns a result that identifies the template, the resolved anchor bindings, and the created node ids keyed by role.
